figures.caption: Figure 1: Existing evaluation rewards stopping near a target viewpoint, but object grounding requires a final viewpoint where the referred object is actually visible and localizable.
Table 1: Comparison with existing embodied navigation datasets. Our dataset is the first to combine all four desirable properties.

Table 2: Plug-in fine-grained navigation module performance on REVERIE-AIM (val-unseen). Standard VLN metrics are fixed per backbone and shown in parentheses beside each backbone name. Bold = best in group; underline = second best.

Figure 2: An overview of the REALM framework. The pipeline comprises three decoupled stages: (i) Navigation: πnav follows instruction I (e.g., “Go to the living room and water the flowers on the table.”) and stops at sTnav ; (ii) Refinement: πref repositions the agent to sTref where the target is proximate and visible; (iii) Grounding: a BERT-based extractor yields a target phrase r̂, passed to OWLv2 for bounding box prediction. The refinement module is plug-and-play across arbitrary VLN backbones.
